your_book:
Wednesday was a rough session for Jaleel's book, with the portfolio sitting on an unrealized loss of $926 (–4.26% overall) and most of the damage concentrated in the two largest software names. ServiceNow cratered –6.47% today — the single worst performer on the day — dragged down by reports that OpenAI is building an AI-agent platform called Presence that could compete directly with enterprise workflow software. That same headline hit Adobe –3.87%, pushing its cumulative loss to –10.9% on a position worth $3,421. Together those two names represent ~27% of the book, so when AI-disruption fear is the narrative du jour, Jaleel's portfolio has no place to hide. The timing is pointed: NOW was already flagged for macro beta risk, and today was literally its next earnings date — any guidance commentary will matter enormously.

The bright spots were narrow but real. Abbott (ABT) added +0.90% and remains the book's cleanest winner at +16.3% unrealized gain; NVIDIA rose +2.30% (+15.6% unrealized) despite chip-sector noise around Intel; and Broadcom ticked up +2.67%. On the drift side, the thesis-vs.-price tension is sharpest in ADBE and BSX: both carry STRONG BUY verdicts and high QARP scores (73.8 and 83.4) yet are down –10.9% and –10.2% respectively from cost, with fundamental headwinds (AI-narrative pressure on Adobe, guidance slashed at BSX) that the ratings haven't fully absorbed. RKLB continues to be the quiet problem — down –23.7% from cost, rated AVOID at a QARP of 43.6, and yet it represents 9.4% of the book.

Watch closely: NOW's earnings catalyst is here today; BSX reports July 29. LULU's guidance-cut flag (Americas comps –5% for the fifth straight quarter) compounds a –2.8% session move. The pharma cluster (GILD, NVS, NBIX, VRTX) faces a drug-pricing policy overhang with the next checkpoint August 6. With cash at $0.38, there is zero dry powder to act on any of this — that alone is worth flagging in a capital-preservation mandate.

attention.headline: Earnings today after –6.47% session selloff — guidance is the only thing that matters now
attention.note: NOW fell –6.47% on AI-agent disruption fears on the same day it reports earnings; the stock's high multiple means any guidance miss or cautious commentary could accelerate the drawdown well beyond today's move.

attention.headline: AVOID-rated, –23.7% underwater, and 9.4% of the book — position conflicts with capital-preservation mandate
attention.note: Rocket Lab carries the lowest QARP score in the portfolio (43.6) and an explicit AVOID verdict, yet it remains the fifth-largest holding by weight with no flags suggesting a thesis recovery is imminent.

attention.headline: Largest holding down –10.9% from cost and –3.87% today on OpenAI Presence reports
attention.note: At 16.4% of the book, Adobe is the single biggest concentration risk, and today's catalyst — a credible AI competitor to its core creative/workflow business — strikes directly at the moat narrative underpinning its STRONG BUY rating.

attention.headline: Guidance already slashed, earnings July 29 — elevated flag with no recovery evidence yet
attention.note: Boston Scientific reports in one week with management guiding flat through Q3; at –10.2% unrealized and a STRONG BUY verdict that the fundamentals currently don't support, the July 29 print is a binary event for this position.
